one_time_description: Perte de valeur des droits de pêche à la crevette (impairment of shrimp fishing rights)
economic_notes: Revenue 57% from government grants (federal and provincial), 31% from fishing revenues. Subsidiaries: Société de développement Wulustuk inc. and Érablière Malécite S.E.C. Joint ventures: Salaweg inc. (33%), Érablière Lac de L'Île inc. (50%), Énergie Éolienne Bas-Saint-Laurent SENC (10%), 9060-7375 Québec inc. (16.67%). Commercial activities include fishing (shrimp, lobster, crab, urchin, sea cucumber), maple syrup production, and warehousing.
notes: French-language consolidated financial statements for a Quebec First Nation. Two fully consolidated subsidiaries: Société de développement Wulustuk inc. and Érablière Malécite S.E.C. Two joint ventures accounted for by modified equity method. Significant one-time impairment of shrimp fishing rights of $1,539,335. Large deferred revenue balance of $7,388,092 primarily from Fisheries and Oceans Canada access to fisheries program ($5,552,149). Federal trust fund balance of $354,799 held in Government of Canada treasury. AANC figure of $3,180,520 on income statement includes both operating ($2,505,928) and capital ($674,592) per note 4. No schedule of remuneration disclosed. Société de développement Wulustuk inc. has unrecognized tax loss carryforwards of $262,940.
